examples: When the children and the scooters have assembled, they trundle to school.
The sun has to trundle across the sky, and they want relief from working and laboring at their assigned stations.
The network has shrunk by roughly a third since its postwar heyday, but more trains now trundle up and down the country than ever before.
Every week 200 children with malaria trundle into the Muheza Designated District Hospital on Tanzania's northern coast.
As buses and cars trundle past behind us, she reflects on her daughter's recent trip to London.
